title: Radiation Therapists
description: Provide radiation therapy to patients as prescribed by a radiation oncologist according to established practices and standards. Duties may include reviewing prescription and diagnosis; acting as liaison with physician and supportive care personnel; preparing equipment, such as immobilization, treatment, and protection devices; and maintaining records, reports, and files. May assist in dosimetry procedures and tumor localization.

tasks.text: Position patients for treatment with accuracy, according to prescription.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer prescribed doses of radiation to specific body parts, using radiation therapy equipment according to established practices and standards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Follow principles of radiation protection for patient, self, and others.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review prescription, diagnosis, patient chart, and identification.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct most treatment sessions independently, in accordance with the long-term treatment plan and under the general direction of the patient's physician.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Enter data into computer and set controls to operate or adjust equipment or regulate dosage.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Check radiation therapy equipment to ensure proper operation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe and reassure patients during treatment and report unusual reactions to physician or turn equipment off if unexpected adverse reactions occur.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate, prepare, and reassure patients and their families by answering questions, providing physical assistance, and reinforcing physicians' advice regarding treatment reactions or post-treatment care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain records, reports, or files as required, including such information as radiation dosages, equipment settings, or patients' reactions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check for side effects, such as skin irritation, nausea, or hair loss to assess patients' reaction to treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare or construct equipment, such as immobilization, treatment, or protection devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Help physicians, radiation oncologists, or clinical physicists to prepare physical or technical aspects of radiation treatment plans, using information about patient condition and anatomy.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Calculate actual treatment dosages delivered during each session.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Photograph treated area of patient and process film.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Act as liaison with physicist and supportive care personnel.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Schedule patients for treatment times.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide assistance to other healthcare personnel during dosimetry procedures and tumor localization.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train or supervise student or subordinate radiotherapy technologists.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Implement appropriate follow-up care plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Store, sterilize, or prepare the special applicators containing the radioactive substance implanted by the physician.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist in the preparation of sealed radioactive materials, such as cobalt, radium, cesium, or isotopes, for use in radiation treatments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
